sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Good morning! May I see your passport and ticket, please?
sentences.text_zh: 早上好！请出示您的护照和机票好吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈmɔːrnɪŋ! meɪ aɪ siː jʊər ˈpɑːspɔːrt ənd ˈtɪkɪt, pliːz?/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Here you are.
sentences.text_zh: 给您。
sentences.phonetic: /hɪər juː ɑːr/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Thank you. Are you checking any baggage today?
sentences.text_zh: 谢谢。您今天有行李需要托运吗？
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː. ɑːr juː ˈtʃekɪŋ ˈeni ˈbæɡɪdʒ təˈdeɪ?/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Yes, I have one suitcase to check in.
sentences.text_zh: 是的，我有一个行李箱要托运。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, aɪ hæv wʌn ˈsuːtkeɪs tuː tʃek ɪn/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Please place it on the scale. Would you prefer a window or aisle seat?
sentences.text_zh: 请把它放在秤上。您想要靠窗还是靠走廊的座位？
sentences.phonetic: /pliːz pleɪs ɪt ɒn ðə skeɪl. wʊd juː prɪˈfɜːr ə ˈwɪndəʊ ɔːr aɪl siːt?/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Window seat, please.
sentences.text_zh: 靠窗座位，谢谢。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈwɪndəʊ siːt, pliːz/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Here's your boarding pass. Boarding starts at 10:30 AM at gate 23. Have a pleasant flight!
sentences.text_zh: 这是您的登机牌。10点30分开始在23号登机口登机。祝您旅途愉快！
sentences.phonetic: /hɪərz jʊər ˈbɔːrdɪŋ pɑːs. ˈbɔːrdɪŋ stɑːrts ət ten ˈθɜːrti eɪ em ət ɡeɪt ˈtwenti θriː. hæv ə ˈplezənt flaɪt!/

cultural_notes: In Western airports, passengers are expected to arrive 2-3 hours before international flights. Always have documents ready before approaching the counter.
practice_tips: Practice the phrase 'Here you are' - it's more natural than 'Here it is' when handing documents to someone.

sentences.speaker: Security
sentences.text_en: Please remove your laptop and liquids from your bag.
sentences.text_zh: 请从包里拿出您的笔记本电脑和液体物品。
sentences.phonetic: /pliːz rɪˈmuːv jʊər ˈlæptɒp ənd ˈlɪkwɪdz frɒm jʊər bæɡ/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Do I need to take off my shoes?
sentences.text_zh: 我需要脱鞋吗？
sentences.phonetic: /duː aɪ niːd tuː teɪk ɒf maɪ ʃuːz?/

sentences.speaker: Security
sentences.text_en: Yes, please. Also remove your belt and empty your pockets.
sentences.text_zh: 是的，请脱下。还请摘下您的皮带并掏空口袋。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, pliːz. ˈɔːlsəʊ rɪˈmuːv jʊər belt ənd ˈempti jʊər ˈpɒkɪts/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Okay, all done.
sentences.text_zh: 好的，都弄好了。
sentences.phonetic: /əʊˈkeɪ, ɔːl dʌn/

sentences.speaker: Security
sentences.text_en: Please step through the metal detector.
sentences.text_zh: 请通过金属探测器。
sentences.phonetic: /pliːz step θruː ðə ˈmetl dɪˈtektər/

sentences.speaker: Security
sentences.text_en: Thank you. You can collect your belongings now.
sentences.text_zh: 谢谢。您现在可以取回您的物品了。
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː. juː kæn kəˈlekt jʊər bɪˈlɒŋɪŋz naʊ/

cultural_notes: TSA (US) and similar security agencies have strict liquid rules: containers must be 100ml or less, placed in a clear plastic bag. Follow instructions promptly to avoid delays.
practice_tips: Practice the phrasal verb 'take off' (remove) vs 'take off' (plane departure) - same words, different meanings based on context.

sentences.speaker: Announcement
sentences.text_en: Attention passengers. We are now boarding rows 20 and above.
sentences.text_zh: 旅客请注意。我们现在开始登机，20排及以后的座位。
sentences.phonetic: /əˈtenʃən ˈpæsɪndʒərz. wiː ɑːr naʊ ˈbɔːrdɪŋ rəʊz ˈtwenti ənd əˈbʌv/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Excuse me, I'm in row 18. Should I wait?
sentences.text_zh: 打扰一下，我是18排的。我应该等一下吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪkˈskjuːz miː, aɪm ɪn rəʊ ˈeɪˈtiːn. ʃʊd aɪ weɪt?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Yes, please wait for your row to be called. We'll board you shortly.
sentences.text_zh: 是的，请等待您的排数被叫到。我们很快会让您登机。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, pliːz weɪt fɔːr jʊər rəʊ tuː biː kɔːld. wiːl bɔːrd juː ˈʃɔːrtli/

sentences.speaker: Announcement
sentences.text_en: We are now inviting rows 10 through 19 to board.
sentences.text_zh: 我们现在邀请10排到19排的乘客登机。
sentences.phonetic: /wiː ɑːr naʊ ɪnˈvaɪtɪŋ rəʊz ten θruː ˈnaɪnˈtiːn tuː bɔːrd/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: May I see your boarding pass, please? You're in seat 18C.
sentences.text_zh: 请出示您的登机牌好吗？您是18C座位。
sentences.phonetic: /meɪ aɪ siː jʊər ˈbɔːrdɪŋ pɑːs, pliːz? jʊər ɪn siːt ˈeɪˈtiːn siː/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Thank you. Which way should I go?
sentences.text_zh: 谢谢。我应该往哪边走？
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː. wɪtʃ weɪ ʃʊd aɪ ɡəʊ?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Turn right and proceed to the rear of the aircraft. Have a nice flight!
sentences.text_zh: 右转然后往飞机后部走。祝您旅途愉快！
sentences.phonetic: /tɜːn raɪt ənd prəˈsiːd tuː ðə rɪər ɒv ði ˈeəkrɑːft. hæv ə naɪs flaɪt!/

cultural_notes: Airlines board by zones or rows to manage flow. Priority boarding often includes first class, business class, families with children, and loyalty program members.
practice_tips: Listen for key boarding announcements: 'rows', 'zones', 'now boarding'. Seat designations follow pattern: Row Number + Letter (e.g., 18C).

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Good evening. I have a reservation under the name Smith.
sentences.text_zh: 晚上好。我有一个预订，名字是Smith。
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈiːvnɪŋ. aɪ hæv ə ˌrezəˈveɪʃən ˈʌndər ðə neɪm smɪθ/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Welcome, Mr. Smith. Let me pull up your reservation. I see you booked a deluxe room for three nights?
sentences.text_zh: 欢迎，Smith先生。让我调出您的预订。我看到您预订了豪华房三晚？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈwelkəm, ˈmɪstər smɪθ. let miː pʊl ʌp jʊər ˌrezəˈveɪʃən. aɪ siː juː bʊkt ə dɪˈlʌks ruːm fɔːr θriː naɪts?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: That's correct. Does the room have a city view?
sentences.text_zh: 没错。房间有城市景观吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ðæts kəˈrekt. dʌz ðə ruːm hæv ə ˈsɪti vjuː?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Yes, you'll be in room 1508 on the 15th floor with a wonderful city view. May I have your ID and credit card, please?
sentences.text_zh: 是的，您将入住15楼的1508房间，有很棒的城市景观。请出示您的身份证件和信用卡好吗？
sentences.phonetic: /jes, juːl biː ɪn ruːm ˈfɪfˈtiːn əʊ eɪt ɒn ðə ˈfɪfˈtiːnθ flɔːr wɪð ə ˈwʌndərfəl ˈsɪti vjuː. meɪ aɪ hæv jʊər aɪ diː ənd ˈkredɪt kɑːrd, pliːz?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Here you go. What time is breakfast served?
sentences.text_zh: 给您。早餐几点供应？
sentences.phonetic: /hɪər juː ɡəʊ. wɒt taɪm ɪz ˈbrekfəst sɜːrvd?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Breakfast is served from 6:30 to 10:00 AM in the restaurant on the ground floor. Here are your room keys. The elevator is to your right.
sentences.text_zh: 早餐从早上6点30分到10点在一楼餐厅供应。这是您的房卡。电梯在您右手边。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈbrekfəst ɪz sɜːrvd frɒm sɪks ˈθɜːrti tuː ten əʊ klɒk eɪ em ɪn ðə ˈrestrɒnt ɒn ðə ɡraʊnd flɔːr. hɪər ɑːr jʊər ruːm kiːz. ði ˈelɪveɪtər ɪz tuː jʊər raɪt/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Thank you very much. Have a good evening!
sentences.text_zh: 非常感谢。祝您晚安！
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː ˈveri mʌtʃ. hæv ə ɡʊd ˈiːvnɪŋ!/

cultural_notes: US: first floor = ground floor. UK: ground floor ≠ first floor (first floor is one level up). Hotels typically require credit card for incidentals even if prepaid.
practice_tips: Learn hotel room types: standard, deluxe, suite. Practice ordinal numbers for floors: 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 15th.

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Hello, this is room 1508. I'd like to order room service, please.
sentences.text_zh: 你好，这是1508房间。我想点客房送餐服务。
sentences.phonetic: /həˈləʊ, ðɪs ɪz ruːm ˈfɪfˈtiːn əʊ eɪt. aɪd laɪk tuː ˈɔːrdər ruːm ˈsɜːrvɪs, pliːz/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Of course, sir. What would you like to have?
sentences.text_zh: 当然，先生。您想要什么呢？
sentences.phonetic: /ɒv kɔːrs, sɜːr. wɒt wʊd juː laɪk tuː hæv?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: I'll have the Caesar salad and a grilled chicken sandwich. Could I also get extra towels?
sentences.text_zh: 我要凯撒沙拉和一个烤鸡三明治。我还能要一些额外的毛巾吗？
sentences.phonetic: /aɪl hæv ðə ˈsiːzər ˈsæləd ənd ə ɡrɪld ˈtʃɪkɪn ˈsænwɪtʃ. kʊd aɪ ˈɔːlsəʊ ɡet ˈekstrə ˈtaʊəlz?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Certainly. Would you like anything to drink with that?
sentences.text_zh: 当然可以。您需要饮料吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈsɜːrtənli. wʊd juː laɪk ˈeniθɪŋ tuː drɪŋk wɪð ðæt?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Yes, a bottle of sparkling water, please. How long will it take?
sentences.text_zh: 是的，请来一瓶气泡水。需要多长时间？
sentences.phonetic: /jes, ə ˈbɒtl ɒv ˈspɑːrklɪŋ ˈwɔːtər, pliːz. haʊ lɒŋ wɪl ɪt teɪk?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: It should arrive in about 20 to 25 minutes. Is there anything else I can help you with?
sentences.text_zh: 大约20到25分钟送到。还有其他需要帮忙的吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪt ʃʊd əˈraɪv ɪn əˈbaʊt ˈtwenti tuː ˈtwenti faɪv ˈmɪnɪts. ɪz ðeər ˈeniθɪŋ els aɪ kæn help juː wɪð?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: No, that's all. Thank you!
sentences.text_zh: 不用了，就这些。谢谢！
sentences.phonetic: /nəʊ, ðæts ɔːl. θæŋk juː!/

cultural_notes: Room service typically includes a delivery fee or service charge (15-20%). Tipping the delivery person $2-5 is customary in the US.
practice_tips: Practice 'I'd like' vs 'I'll have' - both work for ordering, but 'I'd like' is slightly more polite. Learn common food adjectives: grilled, fried, steamed, baked.

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Good morning. I'd like to check out, please. Room 1508.
sentences.text_zh: 早上好。我想退房。1508房间。
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈmɔːrnɪŋ. aɪd laɪk tuː tʃek aʊt, pliːz. ruːm ˈfɪfˈtiːn əʊ eɪt/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Certainly, Mr. Smith. How was your stay?
sentences.text_zh: 当然，Smith先生。您住得怎么样？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈsɜːrtənli, ˈmɪstər smɪθ. haʊ wɒz jʊər steɪ?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: It was excellent, thank you. Could I see my bill before settling up?
sentences.text_zh: 非常好，谢谢。我能在结账前看一下账单吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪt wɒz ˈeksələnt, θæŋk juː. kʊd aɪ siː maɪ bɪl bɪˈfɔːr ˈsetlɪŋ ʌp?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Of course. Let me print that out for you. I see you had room service last night for $45, and there's a minibar charge of $12.
sentences.text_zh: 当然。让我给您打印出来。我看到您昨晚的客房送餐是45美元，还有12美元的迷你吧费用。
sentences.phonetic: /ɒv kɔːrs. let miː prɪnt ðæt aʊt fɔːr juː. aɪ siː juː hæd ruːm ˈsɜːrvɪs lɑːst naɪt fɔːr ˈfɔːrti faɪv ˈdɒlərz, ənd ðeərz ə ˈmɪnibɑːr tʃɑːrdʒ ɒv ˈtwelv ˈdɒlərz/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: That looks correct. Can I pay by credit card?
sentences.text_zh: 看起来是对的。我可以用信用卡支付吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ðæt lʊks kəˈrekt. kæn aɪ peɪ baɪ ˈkredɪt kɑːrd?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Yes, I'll charge it to the card on file. Could you please sign here? Would you like a receipt?
sentences.text_zh: 是的，我会从您登记的卡中扣款。请在这里签名好吗？您需要收据吗？
sentences.phonetic: /jes, aɪl tʃɑːrdʒ ɪt tuː ðə kɑːrd ɒn faɪl. kʊd juː pliːz saɪn hɪər? wʊd juː laɪk ə rɪˈsiːt?/

sentences.speaker: Guest
sentences.text_en: Yes, please. Also, could you call a taxi to the airport?
sentences.text_zh: 是的，请给我。还有，您能帮我叫一辆去机场的出租车吗？
sentences.phonetic: /jes, pliːz. ˈɔːlsəʊ, kʊd juː kɔːl ə ˈtæksi tuː ði ˈeəpɔːrt?/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Absolutely. It should be here in about 5 minutes. Thank you for staying with us!
sentences.text_zh: 当然。大约5分钟就到。感谢您入住我们酒店！
sentences.phonetic: /ˈæbsəluːtli. ɪt ʃʊd biː hɪər ɪn əˈbaʊt faɪv ˈmɪnɪts. θæŋk juː fɔːr ˈsteɪɪŋ wɪð ʌs!/

cultural_notes: Check-out time is typically 11 AM or 12 PM. Late check-out may incur extra charges. Always review your bill for unexpected charges (minibar, phone calls, parking).
practice_tips: Distinguish 'check in' (arrival) vs 'check out' (departure). Learn hotel charges vocabulary: room rate, tax, service charge, incidentals.

sentences.speaker: Person A
sentences.text_en: Good morning, everyone. Let me introduce myself. I'm Sarah Chen, the new Marketing Manager.
sentences.text_zh: 大家早上好。让我自我介绍一下。我是Sarah Chen，新任市场经理。
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈmɔːrnɪŋ, ˈevriwʌn. let miː ˌɪntrəˈdjuːs maɪˈself. aɪm ˈsærə tʃen, ðə njuː ˈmɑːrkɪtɪŋ ˈmænɪdʒər/

sentences.speaker: Person A
sentences.text_en: I joined the company last month from our Singapore office. I'm excited to be working with this team.
sentences.text_zh: 我上个月从新加坡办公室加入公司。很高兴能与这个团队合作。
sentences.phonetic: /aɪ dʒɔɪnd ðə ˈkʌmpəni lɑːst mʌnθ frɒm ˈaʊər ˌsɪŋəˈpɔːr ˈɒfɪs. aɪm ɪkˈsaɪtɪd tuː biː ˈwɜːrkɪŋ wɪð ðɪs tiːm/

sentences.speaker: Person B
sentences.text_en: Welcome aboard, Sarah! I'm Tom Wilson, Sales Director. I've heard great things about your work in Singapore.
sentences.text_zh: 欢迎加入，Sarah！我是Tom Wilson，销售总监。我听说了很多关于您在新加坡工作的好评。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈwelkəm əˈbɔːrd, ˈsærə! aɪm tɒm ˈwɪlsən, seɪlz dəˈrektər. aɪv hɜːrd ɡreɪt θɪŋz əˈbaʊt jʊər wɜːrk ɪn ˌsɪŋəˈpɔːr/

sentences.speaker: Person A
sentences.text_en: Thank you, Tom. I'm looking forward to collaborating with sales on our upcoming campaigns.
sentences.text_zh: 谢谢，Tom。我期待与销售团队在即将到来的营销活动中合作。
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː, tɒm. aɪm ˈlʊkɪŋ ˈfɔːrwərd tuː kəˈlæbəreɪtɪŋ wɪð seɪlz ɒn ˈaʊər ˈʌpkʌmɪŋ kæmˈpeɪnz/

sentences.speaker: Person C
sentences.text_en: Hi Sarah, I'm Linda Park, Product Manager. If you need any support with product information, feel free to reach out.
sentences.text_zh: 你好Sarah，我是Linda Park，产品经理。如果您需要任何产品信息支持，请随时联系我。
sentences.phonetic: /haɪ ˈsærə, aɪm ˈlɪndə pɑːrk, ˈprɒdʌkt ˈmænɪdʒər. ɪf juː niːd ˈeni səˈpɔːrt wɪð ˈprɒdʌkt ˌɪnfərˈmeɪʃən, fiːl friː tuː riːtʃ aʊt/

sentences.speaker: Person A
sentences.text_en: That's very kind of you, Linda. I'm sure I'll take you up on that offer soon!
sentences.text_zh: 您真是太好了，Linda。我肯定很快就会接受您的帮助！
sentences.phonetic: /ðæts ˈveri kaɪnd ɒv juː, ˈlɪndə. aɪm ʃʊər aɪl teɪk juː ʌp ɒn ðæt ˈɒfər suːn/

cultural_notes: In Western business culture, introductions typically include name, title, and a brief background. It's common to offer help to new team members. Handshakes are standard (pre/post-pandemic considerations apply).
practice_tips: Master professional titles: Manager, Director, VP, C-level (CEO, CFO, CTO). Practice 'looking forward to' + gerund structure - very common in business English.

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Alright, let's get started. The main agenda item today is our Q4 marketing strategy. Sarah, would you like to kick things off?
sentences.text_zh: 好的，我们开始吧。今天的主要议程是我们第四季度的市场策略。Sarah，您想先开始吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɔːlˈraɪt, lets ɡet ˈstɑːrtɪd. ðə meɪn əˈdʒendə ˈaɪtəm təˈdeɪ ɪz ˈaʊər kjuː fɔːr ˈmɑːrkɪtɪŋ ˈstrætədʒi. ˈsærə, wʊd juː laɪk tuː kɪk θɪŋz ɒf?/

sentences.speaker: Sarah
sentences.text_en: Absolutely. I've analyzed our current market position and identified three key areas we should focus on.
sentences.text_zh: 当然。我分析了我们当前的市场地位，并确定了我们应该关注的三个关键领域。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈæbsəluːtli. aɪv ˈænəlaɪzd ˈaʊər ˈkʌrənt ˈmɑːrkɪt pəˈzɪʃən ənd aɪˈdentɪfaɪd θriː kiː ˈeəriəz wiː ʃʊd ˈfəʊkəs ɒn/

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text_en: Before we dive in, could you clarify how this aligns with our sales targets?
sentences.text_zh: 在我们深入讨论之前，您能说明一下这如何与我们的销售目标保持一致吗？
sentences.phonetic: /bɪˈfɔːr wiː daɪv ɪn, kʊd juː ˈklærɪfaɪ haʊ ðɪs əˈlaɪnz wɪð ˈaʊər seɪlz ˈtɑːrɡɪts?/

sentences.speaker: Sarah
sentences.text_en: Good question. Each of these initiatives is designed to drive a 15% increase in qualified leads, which should translate to higher conversion rates.
sentences.text_zh: 好问题。这些举措中的每一项都旨在将合格线索增加15%，这应该能转化为更高的转化率。
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈkwestʃən. iːtʃ ɒv ðiːz ɪˈnɪʃətɪvz ɪz dɪˈzaɪnd tuː draɪv ə ˈfɪfˈtiːn pərˈsent ɪnˈkriːs ɪn ˈkwɒlɪfaɪd liːdz, wɪtʃ ʃʊd trænsˈleɪt tuː ˈhaɪər kənˈvɜːrʒən reɪts/

sentences.speaker: Linda
sentences.text_en: That makes sense. However, I'm concerned about the timeline. Can we realistically achieve this by year-end?
sentences.text_zh: 这说得通。但是，我担心时间表。我们能在年底前实际完成这个目标吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ðæt meɪks sens. haʊˈevər, aɪm kənˈsɜːrnd əˈbaʊt ðə ˈtaɪmlaɪn. kæn wiː ˌrɪəˈlɪstɪkli əˈtʃiːv ðɪs baɪ jɪər end?/

sentences.speaker: Sarah
sentences.text_en: Fair point. If we prioritize digital channels and leverage automation tools, I believe it's achievable. We'd need full team buy-in, though.
sentences.text_zh: 说得对。如果我们优先考虑数字渠道并利用自动化工具，我相信是可以实现的。不过，我们需要整个团队的支持。
sentences.phonetic: /feər pɔɪnt. ɪf wiː praɪˈɒrɪtaɪz ˈdɪdʒɪtl ˈtʃænlz ənd ˈliːvərɪdʒ ˌɔːtəˈmeɪʃən tuːlz, aɪ bɪˈliːv ɪts əˈtʃiːvəbl. wiːd niːd fʊl tiːm baɪ ɪn, ðəʊ/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Excellent discussion. Let's table this for now and reconvene after reviewing Sarah's full proposal. Any objections?
sentences.text_zh: 讨论得很好。我们先暂时搁置这个，在审查Sarah的完整提案后再重新开会。有异议吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈeksələnt dɪˈskʌʃən. lets ˈteɪbl ðɪs fɔːr naʊ ənd ˌriːkənˈviːn ˈɑːftər rɪˈvjuːɪŋ ˈsærəz fʊl prəˈpəʊzl. ˈeni əbˈdʒekʃənz?/

cultural_notes: 'Table' means OPPOSITE things in US vs UK! US: postpone discussion. UK: bring up for discussion. In business meetings, disagreements should be expressed politely with phrases like 'I'm concerned...' or 'Could you clarify...'
practice_tips: Master business meeting phrases: 'kick off', 'dive in', 'table this', 'circle back', 'touch base'. Practice softening language: 'I believe', 'It seems', 'Perhaps we could'.

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Alright, let's wrap up. To summarize, we've covered three main points today.
sentences.text_zh: 好的，我们总结一下。总结来说，我们今天讨论了三个要点。
sentences.phonetic: /ɔːlˈraɪt, lets ræp ʌp. tuː ˈsʌməraɪz, wiːv ˈkʌvərd θriː meɪn pɔɪnts təˈdeɪ/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: First, we've agreed on the Q4 marketing strategy focusing on digital channels. Second, the budget allocation has been approved. Third, we've identified potential risks.
sentences.text_zh: 首先，我们同意了专注于数字渠道的第四季度市场策略。其次，预算分配已获批准。第三，我们已经识别了潜在风险。
sentences.phonetic: /fɜːrst, wiːv əˈɡriːd ɒn ðə kjuː fɔːr ˈmɑːrkɪtɪŋ ˈstrætədʒi ˈfəʊkəsɪŋ ɒn ˈdɪdʒɪtl ˈtʃænlz. ˈsekənd, ðə ˈbʌdʒɪt ˌæləˈkeɪʃən həz biːn əˈpruːvd. θɜːrd, wiːv aɪˈdentɪfaɪd pəˈtenʃəl rɪsks/

sentences.speaker: Sarah
sentences.text_en: Just to clarify, the timeline we discussed is still flexible pending final approvals from senior management, correct?
sentences.text_zh: 澄清一下，我们讨论的时间表仍然是灵活的，等待高级管理层的最终批准，对吗？
sentences.phonetic: /dʒʌst tuː ˈklærɪfaɪ, ðə ˈtaɪmlaɪn wiː dɪsˈkʌst ɪz stɪl ˈfleksəbl ˈpendɪŋ ˈfaɪnl əˈpruːvlz frɒm ˈsiːniər ˈmænɪdʒmənt, kəˈrekt?/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: That's right. Moving on to action items: Sarah, you'll draft the detailed proposal by next Friday.
sentences.text_zh: 没错。接下来说行动项：Sarah，您要在下周五之前起草详细提案。
sentences.phonetic: /ðæts raɪt. ˈmuːvɪŋ ɒn tuː ˈækʃən ˈaɪtəmz: ˈsærə, juːl drɑːft ðə dɪˈteɪld prəˈpəʊzl baɪ nekst ˈfraɪdeɪ/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Tom, you're responsible for coordinating with the sales team on lead qualification criteria. Linda, please review the technical feasibility.
sentences.text_zh: Tom，您负责与销售团队协调线索资格标准。Linda，请审查技术可行性。
sentences.phonetic: /tɒm, jʊər rɪˈspɒnsəbl fɔːr kəʊˈɔːrdɪneɪtɪŋ wɪð ðə seɪlz tiːm ɒn liːd ˌkwɒlɪfɪˈkeɪʃən kraɪˈtɪəriə. ˈlɪndə, pliːz rɪˈvjuː ðə ˈteknɪkl ˌfiːzəˈbɪləti/

sentences.speaker: Tom
sentences.text_en: Understood. I'll loop in the regional managers and have preliminary feedback by Wednesday.
sentences.text_zh: 明白了。我会让区域经理参与进来，并在周三之前给出初步反馈。
sentences.phonetic: /ˌʌndərˈstʊd. aɪl luːp ɪn ðə ˈriːdʒənl ˈmænɪdʒərz ənd hæv prɪˈlɪmɪnəri ˈfiːdbæk baɪ ˈwenzdeɪ/

sentences.speaker: Chair
sentences.text_en: Perfect. Let's schedule a follow-up meeting for two weeks from now. I'll send out calendar invites. Thanks everyone for your contributions today!
sentences.text_zh: 很好。我们安排两周后的后续会议。我会发送日历邀请。感谢大家今天的贡献！
sentences.phonetic: /ˈpɜːrfɪkt. lets ˈskedʒuːl ə ˈfɒləʊ ʌp ˈmiːtɪŋ fɔːr tuː wiːks frɒm naʊ. aɪl send aʊt ˈkælɪndər ɪnˈvaɪts. θæŋks ˈevriwʌn fɔːr jʊər ˌkɒntrɪˈbjuːʃənz təˈdeɪ/

cultural_notes: Effective meeting summaries include: key decisions, action items with owners and deadlines, and next steps. Always confirm understanding with phrases like 'Just to clarify...' or 'To confirm...'
practice_tips: Master summary phrases: 'To sum up', 'In conclusion', 'To recap'. Learn action item language: 'You're responsible for', 'Please ensure', 'I'll take care of', 'Let's circle back on'.

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: Good evening! Welcome to The Garden. Here's your menu. Can I start you off with something to drink?
sentences.text_zh: 晚上好！欢迎来到花园餐厅。这是您的菜单。先来点喝的吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˈiːvnɪŋ! ˈwelkəm tuː ðə ˈɡɑːrdən. hɪərz jʊər ˈmenjuː. kæn aɪ stɑːrt juː ɒf wɪð ˈsʌmθɪŋ tuː drɪŋk?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Yes, please. I'll have a glass of sparkling water and my friend would like an iced tea.
sentences.text_zh: 好的。我要一杯气泡水，我朋友想要一杯冰茶。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, pliːz. aɪl hæv ə ɡlɑːs ɒv ˈspɑːrklɪŋ ˈwɔːtər ənd maɪ frend wʊd laɪk ən aɪst tiː/

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: Sure thing. Are you ready to order, or do you need a few more minutes?
sentences.text_zh: 没问题。您准备好点餐了吗，还是需要再看看？
sentences.phonetic: /ʃʊər θɪŋ. ɑːr juː ˈredi tuː ˈɔːrdər, ɔːr duː juː niːd ə fjuː mɔːr ˈmɪnɪts?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: We're ready. I'd like the grilled salmon with a side salad, please.
sentences.text_zh: 我们准备好了。我要烤三文鱼配沙拉。
sentences.phonetic: /wɪər ˈredi. aɪd laɪk ðə ɡrɪld ˈsæmən wɪð ə saɪd ˈsæləd, pliːz/

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: Excellent choice. And how would you like your salmon — medium or well done?
sentences.text_zh: 很好的选择。您的三文鱼要几成熟——五成还是全熟？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈeksələnt tʃɔɪs. ənd haʊ wʊd juː laɪk jʊər ˈsæmən — ˈmiːdiəm ɔːr wel dʌn?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Medium, please. And my friend will have the mushroom risotto.
sentences.text_zh: 五成熟。我朋友要蘑菇烩饭。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈmiːdiəm, pliːz. ənd maɪ frend wɪl hæv ðə ˈmʌʃruːm rɪˈzɒtəʊ/

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: Perfect. I'll put that order in right away. Your drinks will be out shortly.
sentences.text_zh: 好的。我马上去下单。您的饮品很快就来。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈpɜːrfɪkt. aɪl pʊt ðæt ˈɔːrdər ɪn raɪt əˈweɪ. jʊər drɪŋks wɪl biː aʊt ˈʃɔːrtli/

cultural_notes: In Western restaurants, the waiter typically asks how you'd like meat cooked: rare, medium rare, medium, medium well, or well done. Tipping 15-20% is standard in the US.
practice_tips: Master ordering phrases: 'I'll have...', 'I'd like...', 'Could I get...'. Learn cooking terms: grilled, steamed, fried, baked, roasted.

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Excuse me, I have a question about the menu. Are there any nut-free options?
sentences.text_zh: 打扰一下，我想问一下菜单的问题。有不含坚果的选项吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪkˈskjuːz miː, aɪ hæv ə ˈkwestʃən əˈbaʊt ðə ˈmenjuː. ɑːr ðeər ˈeni nʌt friː ˈɒpʃənz?/

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: Of course. Let me check with the kitchen. Do you have a nut allergy?
sentences.text_zh: 当然。让我跟厨房确认一下。您对坚果过敏吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɒv kɔːrs. let miː tʃek wɪð ðə ˈkɪtʃɪn. duː juː hæv ə nʌt ˈælərdʒi?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Yes, I'm severely allergic to peanuts and tree nuts. I also need to avoid gluten if possible.
sentences.text_zh: 是的，我对花生和坚果严重过敏。如果可能的话，我也需要避免麸质。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, aɪm sɪˈvɪərli əˈlɜːrdʒɪk tuː ˈpiːnʌts ənd triː nʌts. aɪ ˈɔːlsəʊ niːd tuː əˈvɔɪd ˈɡluːtən ɪf ˈpɒsəbl/

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: I understand. The grilled chicken breast is both nut-free and gluten-free. We can also prepare the steak without any sauce that contains allergens.
sentences.text_zh: 我明白了。烤鸡胸肉既不含坚果也不含麸质。我们也可以做不加含过敏原酱料的牛排。
sentences.phonetic: /aɪ ˌʌndərˈstænd. ðə ɡrɪld ˈtʃɪkɪn brest ɪz bəʊθ nʌt friː ənd ˈɡluːtən friː. wiː kæn ˈɔːlsəʊ prɪˈpeər ðə steɪk wɪˈðaʊt ˈeni sɔːs ðæt kənˈteɪnz ˈælərdʒənz/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: The chicken sounds great. Could you also make sure the cooking oil doesn't contain any nut products?
sentences.text_zh: 鸡肉听起来不错。您能确保烹饪用油不含任何坚果成分吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ðə ˈtʃɪkɪn saʊndz ɡreɪt. kʊd juː ˈɔːlsəʊ meɪk ʃʊər ðə ˈkʊkɪŋ ɔɪl ˈdʌznt kənˈteɪn ˈeni nʌt ˈprɒdʌkts?/

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: Absolutely. I'll mark your order as allergy-sensitive, and the chef will use separate utensils. We take food allergies very seriously here.
sentences.text_zh: 当然。我会将您的订单标记为过敏敏感，厨师会使用单独的器具。我们非常重视食物过敏问题。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈæbsəluːtli. aɪl mɑːrk jʊər ˈɔːrdər əz ˈælərdʒi ˈsensɪtɪv, ənd ðə ʃef wɪl juːz ˈseprət juːˈtensɪlz. wiː teɪk fuːd ˈælərdʒiz ˈveri ˈsɪəriəsli hɪər/

cultural_notes: Food allergies are taken very seriously in Western countries. Restaurants are legally required to disclose allergens in many countries. Always inform the waiter about any allergies.
practice_tips: Learn allergy vocabulary: 'I'm allergic to...', 'Does this contain...?', 'Is this [dairy/gluten/nut]-free?'. Know common allergens: nuts, dairy, eggs, shellfish, soy, wheat/gluten.

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Excuse me, could we have the bill, please?
sentences.text_zh: 打扰一下，请给我们账单好吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪkˈskjuːz miː, kʊd wiː hæv ðə bɪl, pliːz?/

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: Of course. Here you are. That comes to $68.50 in total.
sentences.text_zh: 当然。给您。总共68.50美元。
sentences.phonetic: /ɒv kɔːrs. hɪər juː ɑːr. ðæt kʌmz tuː ˈsɪksti eɪt ˈdɒlərz ənd ˈfɪfti sents ɪn ˈtəʊtl/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Can we split the bill? We'd like to pay separately.
sentences.text_zh: 我们可以分开付吗？我们想分别付。
sentences.phonetic: /kæn wiː splɪt ðə bɪl? wiːd laɪk tuː peɪ ˈseprətli/

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: Sure, no problem. Would you like me to split it evenly, or by what each person ordered?
sentences.text_zh: 没问题。您要平均分还是按各自点的菜分？
sentences.phonetic: /ʃʊər, nəʊ ˈprɒbləm. wʊd juː laɪk miː tuː splɪt ɪt ˈiːvnli, ɔːr baɪ wɒt iːtʃ ˈpɜːrsən ˈɔːrdərd?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: By what we ordered, please. I'll pay with my credit card.
sentences.text_zh: 按各自点的菜分吧。我用信用卡付。
sentences.phonetic: /baɪ wɒt wiː ˈɔːrdərd, pliːz. aɪl peɪ wɪð maɪ ˈkredɪt kɑːrd/

sentences.speaker: Waiter
sentences.text_en: Here's the card reader. You can tap or insert your card. Would you like to add a tip?
sentences.text_zh: 这是刷卡机。您可以刷卡或插卡。您要加小费吗？
sentences.phonetic: /hɪərz ðə kɑːrd ˈriːdər. juː kæn tæp ɔːr ɪnˈsɜːrt jʊər kɑːrd. wʊd juː laɪk tuː æd ə tɪp?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Yes, I'll add 18%. The food was wonderful. Thank you!
sentences.text_zh: 好的，我加18%小费。菜很好吃。谢谢！
sentences.phonetic: /jes, aɪl æd ˌeɪˈtiːn pəˈsent. ðə fuːd wɒz ˈwʌndərfl. θæŋk juː!/

cultural_notes: Tipping customs vary by country: US 15-20%, UK 10-15%, Australia/Japan no tipping expected. 'Bill' is British English; 'check' is American English. Splitting the bill is common among friends.
practice_tips: Learn payment phrases: 'Can we have the bill?', 'Could I pay by card?', 'Can we split this?'. Know methods: cash, credit card, debit card, contactless/tap, mobile pay.

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Hi, I'm looking for a winter jacket. Do you have anything in a medium?
sentences.text_zh: 你好，我在找冬季外套。有中号的吗？
sentences.phonetic: /haɪ, aɪm ˈlʊkɪŋ fɔːr ə ˈwɪntər ˈdʒækɪt. duː juː hæv ˈeniθɪŋ ɪn ə ˈmiːdiəm?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Yes, we have several styles. Are you looking for something casual or more formal?
sentences.text_zh: 有的，我们有好几种款式。您找休闲款还是正式一点的？
sentences.phonetic: /jes, wiː hæv ˈsevrəl staɪlz. ɑːr juː ˈlʊkɪŋ fɔːr ˈsʌmθɪŋ ˈkæʒuəl ɔːr mɔːr ˈfɔːrml?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Something casual, for everyday wear. And preferably in dark colors — black or navy.
sentences.text_zh: 休闲的，日常穿的。最好是深色——黑色或深蓝色。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈsʌmθɪŋ ˈkæʒuəl, fɔːr ˈevrideɪ weər. ənd ˈprefərəbli ɪn dɑːrk ˈkʌlərz — blæk ɔːr ˈneɪvi/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: How about this one? It's waterproof, very warm, and it's on sale — 30% off the original price.
sentences.text_zh: 这件怎么样？防水又很保暖，而且正在打折——原价七折。
sentences.phonetic: /haʊ əˈbaʊt ðɪs wʌn? ɪts ˈwɔːtərpruːf, ˈveri wɔːrm, ənd ɪts ɒn seɪl — ˈθɜːrti pəˈsent ɒf ðɪ əˈrɪdʒɪnl praɪs/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: That looks nice. Can I try it on? Where's the fitting room?
sentences.text_zh: 看起来不错。我能试穿一下吗？试衣间在哪？
sentences.phonetic: /ðæt lʊks naɪs. kæn aɪ traɪ ɪt ɒn? weərz ðə ˈfɪtɪŋ ruːm?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Of course! The fitting rooms are just around the corner to your right. Take your time.
sentences.text_zh: 当然！试衣间就在右边拐角处。慢慢试。
sentences.phonetic: /ɒv kɔːrs! ðə ˈfɪtɪŋ ruːmz ɑːr dʒʌst əˈraʊnd ðə ˈkɔːrnər tuː jʊər raɪt. teɪk jʊər taɪm/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: It fits perfectly! I'll take it. Do you accept contactless payment?
sentences.text_zh: 正好合适！我要了。你们接受非接触支付吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪt fɪts ˈpɜːrfɪktli! aɪl teɪk ɪt. duː juː əkˈsept ˈkɒntæktləs ˈpeɪmənt?/

cultural_notes: In most Western stores, you can try on clothes before buying. Sales staff may approach to help — it's ok to say 'Just browsing, thanks.' Sizes vary between countries (US, UK, EU).
practice_tips: Learn sizes: XS, S, M, L, XL. Practice 'I'm looking for...', 'Can I try this on?', 'I'll take it'. Know common fabrics: cotton, wool, polyester, silk.

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Hi, I'm interested in buying a new laptop. Could you recommend something for everyday use?
sentences.text_zh: 你好，我想买一台新笔记本电脑。能推荐一款日常使用的吗？
sentences.phonetic: /haɪ, aɪm ˈɪntrəstɪd ɪn ˈbaɪɪŋ ə njuː ˈlæptɒp. kʊd juː ˌrekəˈmend ˈsʌmθɪŋ fɔːr ˈevrideɪ juːs?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Sure! What's your budget? And do you need it mainly for work, streaming, or gaming?
sentences.text_zh: 当然！您的预算是多少？主要用来办公、看视频还是玩游戏？
sentences.phonetic: /ʃʊər! wɒts jʊər ˈbʌdʒɪt? ənd duː juː niːd ɪt ˈmeɪnli fɔːr wɜːrk, ˈstriːmɪŋ, ɔːr ˈɡeɪmɪŋ?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Mostly for work — browsing, emails, and video calls. My budget is around $800.
sentences.text_zh: 主要办公用——上网、收发邮件和视频通话。预算大概800美元。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈməʊstli fɔːr wɜːrk — ˈbraʊzɪŋ, ˈiːmeɪlz, ənd ˈvɪdiəʊ kɔːlz. maɪ ˈbʌdʒɪt ɪz əˈraʊnd eɪt ˈhʌndrəd ˈdɒlərz/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: I'd suggest this model. It has 16 GB of RAM, a 512 GB SSD, and a 14-inch display. The battery life is about 10 hours.
sentences.text_zh: 我推荐这款。它有16GB内存、512GB固态硬盘和14英寸显示屏。电池续航约10小时。
sentences.phonetic: /aɪd səˈdʒest ðɪs ˈmɒdl. ɪt hæz ˌsɪksˈtiːn ˈdʒɪɡəbaɪts ɒv ræm, ə faɪv ˈhʌndrəd ənd twelv ˈdʒɪɡəbaɪt ˌesˌesˈdiː, ənd ə ˈfɔːrˈtiːn ɪntʃ dɪˈspleɪ. ðə ˈbætəri laɪf ɪz əˈbaʊt ten ˈaʊərz/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: That sounds good. Does it come with a warranty?
sentences.text_zh: 听起来不错。有保修吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ðæt saʊndz ɡʊd. dʌz ɪt kʌm wɪð ə ˈwɒrənti?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Yes, it includes a 2-year manufacturer warranty. We also offer an extended warranty for an additional $49.
sentences.text_zh: 是的，包含两年厂家保修。我们还提供额外49美元的延保服务。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, ɪt ɪnˈkluːdz ə tuː jɪər ˌmænjuˈfæktʃərər ˈwɒrənti. wiː ˈɔːlsəʊ ˈɒfər ən ɪkˈstendɪd ˈwɒrənti fɔːr ən əˈdɪʃənl ˈfɔːrti naɪn ˈdɒlərz/

cultural_notes: In electronics stores, don't hesitate to ask for a demo. Many stores offer price matching with competitors. Keep receipts for warranty claims. Extended warranties are optional and often debatable in value.
practice_tips: Learn tech specs vocabulary: RAM, storage, processor, battery life, screen size. Practice comparison: 'This one has more...', 'Which one is faster?', 'What's the difference between...'.

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Hi, I'd like to return this shirt. I bought it last week, but it doesn't fit properly.
sentences.text_zh: 你好，我想退这件衬衫。上周买的，但尺码不合适。
sentences.phonetic: /haɪ, aɪd laɪk tuː rɪˈtɜːrn ðɪs ʃɜːrt. aɪ bɔːt ɪt lɑːst wiːk, bʌt ɪt ˈdʌznt fɪt ˈprɒpəli/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: I'm sorry to hear that. Do you have the receipt and the original tags?
sentences.text_zh: 很抱歉。您有收据和原始标签吗？
sentences.phonetic: /aɪm ˈsɒri tuː hɪər ðæt. duː juː hæv ðə rɪˈsiːt ənd ðɪ əˈrɪdʒɪnl tæɡz?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: Yes, here's the receipt. The tags are still attached. I haven't worn it.
sentences.text_zh: 有的，这是收据。标签还在。我没穿过。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, hɪərz ðə rɪˈsiːt. ðə tæɡz ɑːr stɪl əˈtætʃt. aɪ ˈhævnt wɔːrn ɪt/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Perfect, that's within our 30-day return policy. Would you prefer a refund or an exchange for a different size?
sentences.text_zh: 好的，在我们30天退货政策内。您要退款还是换个尺码？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈpɜːrfɪkt, ðæts wɪˈðɪn ˈaʊər ˈθɜːrti deɪ rɪˈtɜːrn ˈpɒləsi. wʊd juː prɪˈfɜːr ə rɪˈfʌnd ɔːr ən ɪksˈtʃeɪndʒ fɔːr ə ˈdɪfrənt saɪz?/

sentences.speaker: Customer
sentences.text_en: I'd like an exchange, please. Do you have this in a size large?
sentences.text_zh: 我想换一件。有大号的吗？
sentences.phonetic: /aɪd laɪk ən ɪksˈtʃeɪndʒ, pliːz. duː juː hæv ðɪs ɪn ə saɪz lɑːrdʒ?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Let me check. Yes, we do! Here's the large. Would you like to try it on to make sure it fits this time?
sentences.text_zh: 我查一下。有的！这是大号的。您要试穿一下确保这次合适吗？
sentences.phonetic: /let miː tʃek. jes, wiː duː! hɪərz ðə lɑːrdʒ. wʊd juː laɪk tuː traɪ ɪt ɒn tuː meɪk ʃʊər ɪt fɪts ðɪs taɪm?/

cultural_notes: Most stores have a 14-30 day return policy. Keep receipts and original packaging. Some items like underwear or swimwear are usually non-returnable for hygiene reasons.
practice_tips: Learn return phrases: 'I'd like to return/exchange this', 'It doesn't fit/work', 'Can I get a refund?'. Know your rights: receipt, condition, time limit.

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: Hello, I'd like to make an appointment with Dr. Williams, please.
sentences.text_zh: 你好，我想预约Williams医生。
sentences.phonetic: /həˈləʊ, aɪd laɪk tuː meɪk ən əˈpɔɪntmənt wɪð ˈdɒktər ˈwɪljəmz, pliːz/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Sure. Are you a new patient or have you been here before?
sentences.text_zh: 好的。您是新患者还是之前来过？
sentences.phonetic: /ʃʊər. ɑːr juː ə njuː ˈpeɪʃənt ɔːr hæv juː biːn hɪər bɪˈfɔːr?/

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: I've been here once before, about a year ago. My name is David Chen.
sentences.text_zh: 我之前来过一次，大约一年前。我叫David Chen。
sentences.phonetic: /aɪv biːn hɪər wʌns bɪˈfɔːr, əˈbaʊt ə jɪər əˈɡəʊ. maɪ neɪm ɪz ˈdeɪvɪd tʃen/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: Let me pull up your file. What's the reason for your visit today?
sentences.text_zh: 让我调出您的档案。您今天来看什么？
sentences.phonetic: /let miː pʊl ʌp jʊər faɪl. wɒts ðə ˈriːzn fɔːr jʊər ˈvɪzɪt təˈdeɪ?/

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: I've been having headaches for the past two weeks, and they're getting worse.
sentences.text_zh: 过去两周我一直头疼，而且越来越严重。
sentences.phonetic: /aɪv biːn ˈhævɪŋ ˈhedeɪks fɔːr ðə pɑːst tuː wiːks, ənd ðeər ˈɡetɪŋ wɜːrs/

sentences.speaker: Receptionist
sentences.text_en: I see. Dr. Williams has an opening this Thursday at 2:30 PM, or next Monday morning at 10. Which works better for you?
sentences.text_zh: 好的。Williams医生本周四下午2:30有空，或者下周一上午10点。哪个时间更方便？
sentences.phonetic: /aɪ siː. ˈdɒktər ˈwɪljəmz hæz ən ˈəʊpənɪŋ ðɪs ˈθɜːrzdeɪ ət tuː ˈθɜːrti piː em, ɔːr nekst ˈmʌndeɪ ˈmɔːrnɪŋ ət ten. wɪtʃ wɜːrks ˈbetər fɔːr juː?/

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: Thursday at 2:30 would be perfect. Should I bring anything with me?
sentences.text_zh: 周四2:30最好。我需要带什么吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈθɜːrzdeɪ ət tuː ˈθɜːrti wʊd biː ˈpɜːrfɪkt. ʃʊd aɪ brɪŋ ˈeniθɪŋ wɪð miː?/

cultural_notes: In many Western countries, you need to book a doctor's appointment in advance (often days/weeks). Walk-in clinics are available for urgent but non-emergency issues. Bring your insurance card and ID.
practice_tips: Practice scheduling: 'I'd like to make/book/schedule an appointment.' Describe urgency: 'It's urgent', 'As soon as possible', 'Whenever you have availability'.

sentences.speaker: Doctor
sentences.text_en: Good afternoon, David. What brings you in today?
sentences.text_zh: 下午好，David。今天来看什么？
sentences.phonetic: /ɡʊd ˌɑːftərˈnuːn, ˈdeɪvɪd. wɒt brɪŋz juː ɪn təˈdeɪ?/

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: I've been experiencing persistent headaches for about two weeks. They usually start behind my eyes and spread to the back of my head.
sentences.text_zh: 我持续头疼大约两周了。通常从眼睛后面开始，然后蔓延到后脑勺。
sentences.phonetic: /aɪv biːn ɪkˈspɪəriənsɪŋ pəˈsɪstənt ˈhedeɪks fɔːr əˈbaʊt tuː wiːks. ðeɪ ˈjuːʒuəli stɑːrt bɪˈhaɪnd maɪ aɪz ənd spred tuː ðə bæk ɒv maɪ hed/

sentences.speaker: Doctor
sentences.text_en: On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate the pain? And are they constant or do they come and go?
sentences.text_zh: 以1到10的评分，您给疼痛打几分？是持续疼还是时有时无？
sentences.phonetic: /ɒn ə skeɪl ɒv wʌn tuː ten, haʊ wʊd juː reɪt ðə peɪn? ənd ɑːr ðeɪ ˈkɒnstənt ɔːr duː ðeɪ kʌm ənd ɡəʊ?/

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: About a 6 or 7. They come and go, but they've been more frequent lately. I also feel nauseous sometimes.
sentences.text_zh: 大概6或7分。时有时无，但最近越来越频繁了。有时还会恶心。
sentences.phonetic: /əˈbaʊt ə sɪks ɔːr ˈsevən. ðeɪ kʌm ənd ɡəʊ, bʌt ðeɪv biːn mɔːr ˈfriːkwənt ˈleɪtli. aɪ ˈɔːlsəʊ fiːl ˈnɔːʃəs ˈsʌmtaɪmz/

sentences.speaker: Doctor
sentences.text_en: Have you noticed anything that triggers the headaches — like stress, lack of sleep, or certain foods?
sentences.text_zh: 您注意到什么会诱发头疼吗——比如压力、缺乏睡眠或某些食物？
sentences.phonetic: /hæv juː ˈnəʊtɪst ˈeniθɪŋ ðæt ˈtrɪɡərz ðə ˈhedeɪks — laɪk stres, læk ɒv sliːp, ɔːr ˈsɜːrtn fuːdz?/

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: Now that you mention it, they seem to get worse when I've been staring at my computer screen for too long.
sentences.text_zh: 您这么一说，好像盯着电脑屏幕看太久的时候会加重。
sentences.phonetic: /naʊ ðæt juː ˈmenʃən ɪt, ðeɪ siːm tuː ɡet wɜːrs wen aɪv biːn ˈsteərɪŋ ət maɪ kəmˈpjuːtər skriːn fɔːr tuː lɒŋ/

sentences.speaker: Doctor
sentences.text_en: That could be eye strain. I'd like to run a few tests and check your blood pressure. Let's also schedule an eye exam.
sentences.text_zh: 那可能是用眼过度。我想做几项检查并测量您的血压。我们也安排一次眼科检查。
sentences.phonetic: /ðæt kʊd biː aɪ streɪn. aɪd laɪk tuː rʌn ə fjuː tests ənd tʃek jʊər blʌd ˈpreʃər. lets ˈɔːlsəʊ ˈskedʒuːl ən aɪ ɪɡˈzæm/

cultural_notes: Doctors use a 1-10 pain scale universally. Be specific about symptoms: location, duration, frequency, and severity. It's helpful to keep a symptom diary before your visit.
practice_tips: Learn body parts and symptoms: headache, stomachache, sore throat, cough, fever. Practice describing: 'It hurts here', 'The pain is sharp/dull/throbbing', 'It started [time] ago'.

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: Hello, I have a prescription from Dr. Williams. Here it is.
sentences.text_zh: 你好，我有Williams医生开的处方。给您。
sentences.phonetic: /həˈləʊ, aɪ hæv ə prɪˈskrɪpʃən frɒm ˈdɒktər ˈwɪljəmz. hɪər ɪt ɪz/

sentences.speaker: Pharmacist
sentences.text_en: Thank you. Let me fill this for you. It should take about 10 minutes. Do you have any drug allergies?
sentences.text_zh: 谢谢。让我给您配药。大约需要10分钟。您有药物过敏吗？
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː. let miː fɪl ðɪs fɔːr juː. ɪt ʃʊd teɪk əˈbaʊt ten ˈmɪnɪts. duː juː hæv ˈeni drʌɡ ˈælərdʒiz?/

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: No known allergies. How should I take this medication?
sentences.text_zh: 没有已知的过敏。这个药怎么吃？
sentences.phonetic: /nəʊ nəʊn ˈælərdʒiz. haʊ ʃʊd aɪ teɪk ðɪs ˌmedɪˈkeɪʃən?/

sentences.speaker: Pharmacist
sentences.text_en: Take one tablet twice a day with food — once in the morning and once in the evening. Don't take it on an empty stomach.
sentences.text_zh: 每天随餐服用两次，每次一片——早上一次，晚上一次。不要空腹服用。
sentences.phonetic: /teɪk wʌn ˈtæblɪt twaɪs ə deɪ wɪð fuːd — wʌns ɪn ðə ˈmɔːrnɪŋ ənd wʌns ɪn ðɪ ˈiːvnɪŋ. dəʊnt teɪk ɪt ɒn ən ˈempti ˈstʌmək/

sentences.speaker: Patient
sentences.text_en: Are there any side effects I should be aware of?
sentences.text_zh: 有什么我应该注意的副作用吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɑːr ðeər ˈeni saɪd ɪˈfekts aɪ ʃʊd biː əˈweər ɒv?/

sentences.speaker: Pharmacist
sentences.text_en: You might feel a bit drowsy, so avoid driving until you know how it affects you. If you experience any rash or swelling, stop taking it and call your doctor immediately.
sentences.text_zh: 您可能会有些嗜睡，所以在了解药物反应前避免驾驶。如果出现皮疹或肿胀，请停药并立即联系医生。
sentences.phonetic: /juː maɪt fiːl ə bɪt ˈdraʊzi, səʊ əˈvɔɪd ˈdraɪvɪŋ ʌnˈtɪl juː nəʊ haʊ ɪt əˈfekts juː. ɪf juː ɪkˈspɪəriəns ˈeni ræʃ ɔːr ˈswelɪŋ, stɒp ˈteɪkɪŋ ɪt ənd kɔːl jʊər ˈdɒktər ɪˈmiːdiətli/

cultural_notes: In many countries, pharmacists can advise on minor ailments without a prescription. Over-the-counter (OTC) medicines don't need a prescription. Always finish the full course of antibiotics.
practice_tips: Learn medicine forms: tablet, capsule, syrup, cream, drops. Frequency: once/twice/three times a day. Before/after meals, with water. Key phrases: 'How do I take this?', 'Can I take this with other medicine?'.

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Excuse me, I'd like to buy a ticket to Central Station, please.
sentences.text_zh: 打扰一下，我想买一张到中央车站的票。
sentences.phonetic: /ɪkˈskjuːz miː, aɪd laɪk tuː baɪ ə ˈtɪkɪt tuː ˈsentrəl ˈsteɪʃən, pliːz/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Single or return? And are you traveling today?
sentences.text_zh: 单程还是往返？您今天出行吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ˈsɪŋɡl ɔːr rɪˈtɜːrn? ənd ɑːr juː ˈtrævəlɪŋ təˈdeɪ?/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: A return ticket, please. Yes, I'm traveling today and coming back this evening.
sentences.text_zh: 往返票。是的，今天去今晚回来。
sentences.phonetic: /ə rɪˈtɜːrn ˈtɪkɪt, pliːz. jes, aɪm ˈtrævəlɪŋ təˈdeɪ ənd ˈkʌmɪŋ bæk ðɪs ˈiːvnɪŋ/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: That'll be $12.50. You can pay by cash or card. The next train departs from platform 3 at 10:15.
sentences.text_zh: 12.50美元。您可以现金或刷卡支付。下一班火车10:15从3号站台出发。
sentences.phonetic: /ðætl biː twelv ˈdɒlərz ənd ˈfɪfti sents. juː kæn peɪ baɪ kæʃ ɔːr kɑːrd. ðə nekst treɪn dɪˈpɑːrts frɒm ˈplætfɔːrm θriː ət ten ˈfɪftiːn/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Great, I'll pay by card. Is the train usually on time?
sentences.text_zh: 好的，我刷卡。火车通常准时吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ɡreɪt, aɪl peɪ baɪ kɑːrd. ɪz ðə treɪn ˈjuːʒuəli ɒn taɪm?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Yes, it's generally quite reliable. Here's your ticket. Don't forget to tap it at the gates when you enter and exit.
sentences.text_zh: 是的，一般都挺准时的。这是您的票。进出站时别忘了在闸机上刷一下。
sentences.phonetic: /jes, ɪts ˈdʒenrəli kwaɪt rɪˈlaɪəbl. hɪərz jʊər ˈtɪkɪt. dəʊnt fərˈɡet tuː tæp ɪt ət ðə ɡeɪts wen juː ˈentər ənd ˈeksɪt/

cultural_notes: British English uses 'single' and 'return'; American English uses 'one-way' and 'round-trip'. Many cities now use contactless cards (Oyster, Octopus, Metro). Always validate your ticket.
practice_tips: Learn ticket types: single/one-way, return/round-trip, day pass, weekly pass. Know transport vocabulary: platform, gate, track, departure, arrival.

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Excuse me, how do I get to the Natural History Museum from here?
sentences.text_zh: 打扰一下，从这里怎么去自然历史博物馆？
sentences.phonetic: /ɪkˈskjuːz miː, haʊ duː aɪ ɡet tuː ðə ˈnætʃrəl ˈhɪstri mjuːˈziːəm frɒm hɪər?/

sentences.speaker: Local
sentences.text_en: You'll need to take the Blue Line heading north to Park Street, and then transfer to the Green Line.
sentences.text_zh: 您需要乘坐北行的蓝线到公园街站，然后换乘绿线。
sentences.phonetic: /juːl niːd tuː teɪk ðə bluː laɪn ˈhedɪŋ nɔːrθ tuː pɑːrk striːt, ənd ðen ˈtrænsfɜːr tuː ðə ɡriːn laɪn/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Which platform do I need for the Blue Line?
sentences.text_zh: 蓝线在哪个站台？
sentences.phonetic: /wɪtʃ ˈplætfɔːrm duː aɪ niːd fɔːr ðə bluː laɪn?/

sentences.speaker: Local
sentences.text_en: Go down those stairs and it's on your left — platform B. You should see signs for 'Northbound'.
sentences.text_zh: 走下那个楼梯，在您左边——B站台。您应该能看到'北行'的标志。
sentences.phonetic: /ɡəʊ daʊn ðəʊz steərz ənd ɪts ɒn jʊər left — ˈplætfɔːrm biː. juː ʃʊd siː saɪnz fɔːr ˈnɔːrθbaʊnd/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: How many stops is it to Park Street?
sentences.text_zh: 到公园街有几站？
sentences.phonetic: /haʊ ˈmeni stɒps ɪz ɪt tuː pɑːrk striːt?/

sentences.speaker: Local
sentences.text_en: Just three stops. When you get to Park Street, follow the signs to the Green Line. The museum is two stops from there — you can't miss it!
sentences.text_zh: 只有三站。到公园街后，跟着绿线的标志走。博物馆离那里两站——不会错过的！
sentences.phonetic: /dʒʌst θriː stɒps. wen juː ɡet tuː pɑːrk striːt, ˈfɒləʊ ðə saɪnz tuː ðə ɡriːn laɪn. ðə mjuːˈziːəm ɪz tuː stɒps frɒm ðeər — juː kɑːnt mɪs ɪt!/

cultural_notes: Metro/subway maps use color coding for different lines. 'Northbound/Southbound/Eastbound/Westbound' indicate direction. Ask station staff for help — they usually have free maps.
practice_tips: Learn direction words: north/south/east/west, left/right, straight ahead, up/down stairs. Practice: 'How do I get to...?', 'Which line do I take?', 'Where do I transfer?'.

sentences.speaker: Announcement
sentences.text_en: Attention passengers. We regret to inform you that the 10:15 service to Central Station has been delayed by approximately 20 minutes due to a signal failure.
sentences.text_zh: 各位乘客请注意。我们很遗憾地通知您，10:15开往中央车站的列车因信号故障延误约20分钟。
sentences.phonetic: /əˈtenʃən ˈpæsɪndʒərz. wiː rɪˈɡret tuː ɪnˈfɔːrm juː ðæt ðə ten ˈfɪftiːn ˈsɜːrvɪs tuː ˈsentrəl ˈsteɪʃən hæz biːn dɪˈleɪd baɪ əˈprɒksɪmətli ˈtwenti ˈmɪnɪts djuː tuː ə ˈsɪɡnl ˈfeɪljər/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Excuse me, is there another way to get to Central Station? I have a meeting at noon and I can't be late.
sentences.text_zh: 打扰一下，有没有其他方式去中央车站？我中午有个会议，不能迟到。
sentences.phonetic: /ɪkˈskjuːz miː, ɪz ðeər əˈnʌðər weɪ tuː ɡet tuː ˈsentrəl ˈsteɪʃən? aɪ hæv ə ˈmiːtɪŋ ət nuːn ənd aɪ kɑːnt biː leɪt/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: You could take the express bus from stop C — it goes directly to Central Station and should get you there in about 25 minutes.
sentences.text_zh: 您可以在C站乘坐快线巴士——直达中央车站，大约25分钟到达。
sentences.phonetic: /juː kʊd teɪk ðɪ ɪkˈspres bʌs frɒm stɒp siː — ɪt ɡəʊz dɪˈrektli tuː ˈsentrəl ˈsteɪʃən ənd ʃʊd ɡet juː ðeər ɪn əˈbaʊt ˈtwenti faɪv ˈmɪnɪts/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: That works. Where is stop C? And can I use my train ticket on the bus?
sentences.text_zh: 那行。C站在哪？我能用火车票坐巴士吗？
sentences.phonetic: /ðæt wɜːrks. weər ɪz stɒp siː? ənd kæn aɪ juːz maɪ treɪn ˈtɪkɪt ɒn ðə bʌs?/

sentences.speaker: Staff
sentences.text_en: Exit through the main entrance and turn left. The bus stop is about 50 meters down the road. And yes, your train ticket is valid for the replacement bus service.
sentences.text_zh: 从正门出去左转。公交站在路前方约50米处。是的，您的火车票在替代公交服务上有效。
sentences.phonetic: /ˈeksɪt θruː ðə meɪn ˈentrəns ənd tɜːrn left. ðə bʌs stɒp ɪz əˈbaʊt ˈfɪfti ˈmiːtərz daʊn ðə rəʊd. ənd jes, jʊər treɪn ˈtɪkɪt ɪz ˈvælɪd fɔːr ðə rɪˈpleɪsmənt bʌs ˈsɜːrvɪs/

sentences.speaker: Passenger
sentences.text_en: Thank you so much for your help. Can I get a delay certificate for my employer?
sentences.text_zh: 非常感谢您的帮助。我能拿一份延误证明给雇主吗？
sentences.phonetic: /θæŋk juː səʊ mʌtʃ fɔːr jʊər help. kæn aɪ ɡet ə dɪˈleɪ sərˈtɪfɪkət fɔːr maɪ ɪmˈplɔɪər?/

cultural_notes: Delays are common in public transport. Most systems offer replacement services. In the UK, you can claim compensation for delays over 15-30 minutes. Delay certificates can excuse late arrival at work.
practice_tips: Learn delay phrases: 'How long will the delay be?', 'Is there an alternative route?', 'Can I get a refund?'. Understand announcements: 'due to', 'approximately', 'we apologize for the inconvenience'.
